diffCoverage.notes: Reconciliation: shipped in the multi-account merge; contract reworded to match the IncomingMessage.account_id tagging design, diagnostic added, live no-leak proof captured.

findings.evidence: The captured live log shows both directions of the no-leak invariant: 'route: msg account=1483... -> background-bucket (active=2299...)' and 'route: msg account=2299... -> background-bucket (active=1483...)'. Events are tagged at the source (IncomingMessage.account_id) and routed by the ui store keyed on it. cargo test (listener mapping) + svelte-check are attested; security + architecture reviewers pass.

summary: per-account-routing is honestly done. Events carry the receiving account on IncomingMessage.account_id (the planned AppEvent design realized inline), and the frontend store routes strictly by it — a background account's message lands in its own bucket and never enters the active account's view, proven live in both directions. types/route covered by build + tests; the no-leak smoke is captured. Pass.
